interpretation: From the end user's perspective, this tool should feel like talking to a knowledgeable product manager who asks the right questions and produces a spec the user feels confident handing off to a development team.

must: Present spec in human-readable format with clear section headings
Highlight decisions that need user input with suggested defaults
Allow users to approve, reject, or provide targeted feedback
should: Show which model contributed each requirement for transparency
Converge within 2-3 feedback rounds for typical ideas
could: Provide a diff view between spec versions
wont: Require users to understand the underlying model architecture

assumptions.assumption: Users prefer structured decisions over open-ended prompts
Most ideas can be adequately specified in 1-3 pages

open_questions.question: Should the tool explain why certain requirements were prioritised differently?

risks.risk: Users may feel overwhelmed by too many decisions at once
risks.suggestion: Batch decisions and surface only critical ones first

risks.risk: Feedback loop feels slow due to re-expansion
risks.suggestion: Skip re-expansion for minor feedback, re-synthesise only

success_criteria: User can go from raw idea to approved spec in under 10 minutes
Spec includes all information needed to start development without further clarification
